html:
Riskified Ltd. (9TN.F / RSKD) - The Fraud Underwriter Becoming a Cash-Flow Compounder

Riskified is not a normal software story. It is an ecommerce risk underwriter with software distribution, network data, and a shrinking share count. The company reviews merchant GMV, approves the orders it is willing to stand behind, earns a risk-adjusted fee on approved volume, and reimburses the merchant if an approved transaction becomes fraud chargeback loss. That structure matters because Riskified is paid to say yes more accurately, not just to sell a dashboard.

The market still prices it like a broken 2021 growth IPO with single-digit revenue growth and persistent GAAP losses. That framing is stale. In Q1 2026, Riskified reported GMV up 9%, revenue up 7%, GAAP gross profit up 13%, adjusted EBITDA up to $6.2 million from $1.3 million, free cash flow up to $9.0 million from $3.6 million, and cash, deposits, and investments of $276.3 million with zero debt. Management also raised 2026 guidance to $376-$384 million of revenue and $28-$34 million of adjusted EBITDA. The key is not that revenue is suddenly hyper-growth. The key is that gross profit, EBITDA, free cash flow, and per-share value are inflecting faster than revenue.

The old “$500 million net cash box” thesis is now wrong, but in a useful way. Riskified did not just sit on cash. It bought back stock aggressively. During 2025 it repurchased about 22 million shares for $105.9 million, and since the buyback began in late 2023 it had repurchased about 52 million shares for $259.5 million, reducing shares outstanding by 17%. In Q1 2026 it bought another 6.2 million shares for $27.5 million at an average price of $4.44. At March 31, 2026, actual Class A plus Class B shares outstanding were about 144.0 million. At the current primary RSKD price of about $4.61, or roughly EUR 3.96 equivalent using EUR/USD near 1.163, the equity value is about $664 million and enterprise value is only about $388 million.

That means the stock trades at roughly 1.0x 2026 revenue, 12.5x 2026 adjusted EBITDA, and under 10x expected 2026 free cash flow on an ex-cash basis if the company reaches the roughly $40 million free cash flow expectation it gave coming into the year. For a debt-free company with a real network-data asset, improving unit economics, and an active buyback, that is too cheap.

The variant perception is that Riskified’s real earnings power is hidden by three things: seasonal quarterly optics, accounting noise from share-based compensation, and the market’s failure to distinguish low revenue growth from high per-share profit growth.

Q4 2025 already showed the shape of the mature model: revenue of $99.3 million, GAAP gross margin of 57%, GAAP profitability, and 18% adjusted EBITDA margin. Q1 naturally stepped down seasonally, but the underlying progress remained clear. Revenue grew $5.9 million year over year while cost of revenue grew only $0.4 million. Almost the entire incremental revenue dollar became gross profit. Non-GAAP operating expenses were nearly flat, and constant-currency non-GAAP operating expenses declined 7%. This is what operating leverage looks like before the market gives the company credit for it.

The business transition is also underappreciated. The old Riskified story was Chargeback Guarantee. The new Riskified story is a broader risk-intelligence platform across checkout fraud, disputes, returns abuse, policy abuse, identity intelligence, and agentic commerce. In Q1, the number of merchants using more than one Riskified product grew about 50% year over year. Three of its top ten deals were in ACH, including its largest new logo win, which extends the model beyond traditional card transactions. It also launched Dispute Resolve for Shopify merchants, partnered with Radial, deepened airline distribution through Outpayce from Amadeus, and expanded identity intelligence through Rue Gilt Groupe.

The agentic AI angle is not marketing fluff. Riskified’s April 2026 consumer survey found 61.5% of consumers had used AI tools for product discovery or recommendations, but 55.0% were not comfortable with AI agents making purchases on their behalf and 53.9% believed AI could increase online fraud risk. At Ascend 2026, Riskified introduced ARIA, Identity Explore 2.0, and Decision Studio enhancements, tools that expose network identity patterns, explain risk decisions, and let fraud teams deploy rules from Riskified’s data. If AI shopping agents increase both false declines and fraud sophistication, merchants will need systems that can distinguish trusted automation from malicious automation. Riskified is positioned exactly at that decision layer.

Retail and social context are favorable because there is almost no crowd in the trade. Stocktwits shows RSKD sentiment and message volume as N/A, not euphoric. Reddit attention is mostly old 2021 IPO-era due diligence or automated earnings posts. This is not a meme premium. It is a small, foreign-issuer, under-followed fintech infrastructure name where the share count is shrinking while the market is still anchored to a stale “slow-growth unprofitable software” narrative.

The main risk is underwriting. If fraud loss ratios worsen, gross margin compresses and the whole operating leverage thesis breaks. The second risk is merchant concentration and timing of new merchant go-lives, which can make quarterly revenue lumpy. The third is competition from Signifyd, Forter, Accertify, Sift, payment processors, and large merchants building more in-house fraud systems. The fourth is capital allocation: buybacks below intrinsic value are excellent, but a large acquisition could dilute the clean thesis. There is also Israel-related operational risk and the usual foreign-private-issuer governance discount.

But the downside is no longer “cash burn software stock.” It is a debt-free business doing almost $380 million of forward revenue, generating positive free cash flow, buying back stock, and holding $276 million of cash and investments. Even if growth disappoints, the balance sheet and buyback give management time and options.

The base case is not heroic. Revenue compounds low double digits into 2027, gross margin trends into the mid-to-high 50s as model performance improves, adjusted EBITDA approaches $55-$65 million, free cash flow moves toward $55 million, and buybacks retire another 8-12% of the company over the next two years. Put a 15x FCF multiple on that, add residual cash, divide by a lower share count, and the stock is worth around $8.80, or about EUR 7.60 for 9TN.F.

The bull case is that Riskified is no longer valued as fraud software, but as a profitable risk-decision network for ecommerce, ACH, returns, disputes, and AI-agent commerce. If revenue reaches $475-$500 million over the next cycle with 18-20% EBITDA margins, the operating business can support a 3.0x revenue or high-teens FCF multiple. With continued repurchases, that gets to roughly $14 per RSKD share, or about EUR 12.00.

Current price: about EUR 3.96 for 9TN.F equivalent, based on RSKD at about $4.61 and EUR/USD near 1.163.

Bear target: EUR 2.70, down 32%. Growth stalls, gross margins stay near 50%, buybacks slow, and the market values the operating business at a distressed multiple despite the cash.

Base target: EUR 7.60, up 92%. The market recognizes 2026 as the free-cash-flow inflection year, gives Riskified a modest profitable-fintech multiple, and continued buybacks lift per-share value.

Bull target: EUR 12.00, up 203%. Multi-product adoption, ACH, policy abuse, and agentic-commerce risk turn Riskified back into a durable double-digit grower with real operating leverage.
